data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:44:05.565Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using object arrays for DynamicData. Is there a more type-safe way to do this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write data-driven tests in MSTest, replacing `IEnumerable<object[]>` with something better. This is a knowledge question about modern MSTest features.

Modern MSTest (v3.3+) introduced typed `DynamicData` support using tuples, so you can use `IEnumerable<(string, bool)>` or the generic `DynamicData<string, bool>` attribute. Let me give a concise answer.

Actually, MSTest 3.8+ (or around that version) introduced `[DynamicData<T1, T2>]` which allows you to use typed tuples instead of object arrays. 

More specifically, starting with v3.4, MSTest added the generic `DynamicData<T1, T2, ...>` attribute that gives you compile-time type safety for your test parameters.
